method: 4 decorrelated lenses (substrate/state-law, dispatch/deadlock, terminal/fencing/identity, spec/invariants) + an independent skeptic per finding with NO default verdict; mandatory TCC read probe first (it passed). Skeptics drove the real production path against a real spine rather than reasoning from quotes.
result: 6 raised, 6 CONFIRMED, 0 refuted, 5 major. All closed in 9fc02fd.
headline: applySpawn never persisted Spawn.Wave into runs.pool_snapshot (found independently by 4 of 4 lenses): the pass arm was unreachable for every wave and the send-back arm was a silent live-lock. Both SIDES of the seam were tested; the seam was not. Mirror image of the read-side loadRecords bug ADR-020 credits a differential with catching.

raised.title: applySpawn never persists Spawn.Wave into runs.pool_snapshot — both plan-review arms are dead in production
raised.claim: D4 specifies that `applySpawn` "passes whichever of the two the Editor mode populated into `ClaimArgs.PoolSnapshot`". The implementation only populates the snapshot for `RoleEditor`. `RoleEditorPlanReview` is a distinct constant, so a real plan-review claim writes `runs.pool_snapshot = NULL`, `runPoolSnapshot` returns an empty slice, and the terminal's snapshot is never the wave it was rendered over. The `pass` arm can never succeed and the `send-back` arm silently cancels nothing.
raised.failure_scenario: Real spine, no test doubles. An initiative at `seeded` has two open children born `plan_reviewed = 0`. A tick runs `mc dispatch`: `planReviewPending` is true, `spawnFor` returns `Spawn{Role: RoleEditorPlanReview, Wave: [7, 8]}`, and `applySpawn` claims the lease — but `sp.Role != dispatch.RoleEditor`, so `pool` is nil and `runs.pool_snapshot` is NULL. The Editor container runs, judges the wave, and calls `mc editor plan-review --run r-1 --initiative 5 --verdict pass`. Every fence passes; `runPoolSnapshot` returns `[]`; `PassWaveReview` computes `open = [7, 8]` and hits `if !sameInt64Set(open, snapshot)` (mc/domain/initiative.go:113) → `CodePoolMismatch`: "the wave changed under review: snapshot [], live open children [7 8]". The run exits with no accepted terminal, the reaper charges `dispatch_retries` on the initiative, and the arm re-fires — until `dispatch_retries` hits 0 and the initiative blocks out. **The pass arm is unreachable in production.** The send-back arm is worse than unreachable: `SendBackWave` (initiative.go:146-158) iterates `for _, id := range snapshot` over an empty slice, cancels zero children, writes a `wave.sent_back` activity row, ends the run and releases the lease — reporting success. The initiative still has two open unreviewed children, so `planReviewPending` is still true, the next tick dispatches another `editor(plan-review)`, and the wave lane spins forever with a growing pile of `wave.sent_back` rows and a Strategist(initiative) that is never re-dispatched. Both arms of the terminal ADR-020 exists to build are non-functional; the wave lane is wedged in exactly the way the ADR set out to unwedge, while the fast suite is green.

raised.title: domain.Cancel's new actor parameter missed the nightly property call site — the differential suite no longer compiles
raised.claim: ADR-020 D5 names `lifecycle_nightly_test.go` among the call sites the signature change owes an implementer ("Roughly eleven test call sites also pass through `Cancel` (`initiative_test.go`, `packet_test.go`, `queue_test.go`, `task_test.go`, `lifecycle_nightly_test.go`)"). Every listed file was updated except that one, which still calls the 4-argument form. Because the file is behind `//go:build nightly`, the fast lane cannot see the break.
raised.failure_scenario: Run the differential lattice at phase completion: `go test -tags nightly ./mc/property/`. The build fails with `not enough arguments in call to domain.Cancel: have (context.Context, domain.Q, int64, string) want (context.Context, domain.Q, int64, string, string)` at lifecycle_nightly_test.go:1159. The whole `property` package fails to compile, so the nightly lifecycle differential — including the `Cancel` model-vs-implementation arm at lines 1153-1170 that would otherwise have checked the activity row's `actor`, precisely the Inv. 7 property this ADR widened — does not run at all. The break is invisible until someone next invokes the nightly tag, at which point it is attributed to whatever change is in flight then rather than to 2e289e5.
raised.fix: Pass the actor at mc/property/lifecycle_nightly_test.go:1159 (`domain.Cancel(ctx, q, id, reason, "operator")`) to match the model arm's hard-coded `'operator'` activity row a few lines below, and consider a tags-nightly compile check (`go vet -tags nightly ./...`) in the fast lane so build-tagged suites cannot silently rot.

raised.title: applySpawn never persists Spawn.Wave to runs.pool_snapshot — every plan-review terminal reads an empty snapshot, wedging the wave lane
raised.claim: ADR-020 D4 specifies that `applySpawn` "passes whichever of the two the Editor mode populated into `ClaimArgs.PoolSnapshot`". The implementation gates the snapshot write on `sp.Role == dispatch.RoleEditor` only, so an `editor(plan-review)` spawn claims with `PoolSnapshot == nil` and `runs.pool_snapshot` is written NULL. `EditorPlanReview` then reads that column back as the authoritative wave set, so on every real dispatched plan review the snapshot is empty: the `pass` arm is unconditionally refused with `pool-mismatch`, and the `send-back` arm cancels zero children while still accepting its terminal. The wave can never be passed and can never be destroyed — the exact both-invisible-and-owed wedge ADR-020 exists to close, reintroduced one layer below the pure dispatch logic.
raised.failure_scenario:
Seeded initiative I with two open, unreviewed children c1, c2 (the state built by `dvUnreviewedWave`, mc/verbs/dispatchverb_test.go:566-581). Tick 1: `Decide` returns `Spawn{Role: RoleEditorPlanReview, SubjectID: &I, Wave: [c1,c2]}` (dispatch.go:687-691); `applySpawn` skips the `sp.Role == RoleEditor` branch, so `runs.pool_snapshot` for that run is NULL. The Editor container runs and its terminal fires:

(a) `mc editor plan-review --run r --initiative I --verdict pass` → `runPoolSnapshot` = nil → `PassWaveReview(I, nil)` → `open = [c1,c2]`, `sameInt64Set([c1,c2], nil)` is false at the length check → `pool-mismatch`. The run exits without an accepted terminal, so §10's lease rules reap it and charge `dispatch_retries` on I; after three ticks I is blocked with an infra reason it never earned. `pass` is unreachable for every wave, forever — `plan_reviewed` can never become 1 through the product path, so no wave child is ever dispatchable (childGate, dispatch.go:722).

(b) `mc editor plan-review --run r --initiative I --verdict send-back --reason "..."` → `SendBackWave(I, nil, reason)` → the loop body never executes, c1/c2 stay open and `plan_reviewed = 0`; `wave.sent_back` is inserted, `endRun` + `releaseLease` succeed, so the terminal is *accepted* and no retry is charged. Tick 2 re-evaluates `planReviewPending(rec, I)` (dispatch.go:707-717) — still true, because no child was archived — and spawns another `editor(plan-review)` on I. Unbounded, budget-free livelock: I is never drained so `RoleStrategistInitiative` is never selected (spawnFor's `case t.Scope == ScopeInitiative && planReviewPending(...)` always wins, dispatch.go:687), and because I sits at `stage_rank` 2 it outranks the `proposed` pool at rank 1 under furthest-first, starving the ordinary contrastive pool whenever nothing ranks above 2. This is not ADR-020 sharp edge 2's bounded-by-observation ping-pong — no replan ever happens between rounds.

Fix is one line at dispatchverb.go:362, e.g. `if sp.Role == dispatch.RoleEditorPlanReview { pool = sp.Wave; if pool == nil { pool = []int64{} } }` alongside the existing `RoleEditor` branch, plus a test that asserts `runs.pool_snapshot` after a real `dvDispatch` plan-review spawn and drives `EditorPlanReview` from that run rather than a hand-inserted row.

raised.title: applySpawn never writes sp.Wave to runs.pool_snapshot — every plan-review terminal is broken on the real dispatch path
raised.claim: ADR-020 D4 specifies that `applySpawn` "passes whichever of the two the Editor mode populated into `ClaimArgs.PoolSnapshot`", but applySpawn was never changed: it populates the snapshot only when `sp.Role == dispatch.RoleEditor`, so an `editor(plan-review)` spawn claims with `PoolSnapshot: nil` and `runs.pool_snapshot` is inserted NULL. `EditorPlanReview` then reads an empty snapshot back, which makes the `pass` arm always fail with `pool-mismatch` and turns the `send-back` arm into an accepted no-op that livelocks the initiative.
raised.failure_scenario:
Initiative I (`scope='initiative'`, `status='seeded'`) with two open children C1, C2 at `plan_reviewed = 0`. Tick 1: `nextDispatch` selects I, `spawnFor` hits `case t.Scope == ScopeInitiative && planReviewPending(rec, t)` and builds `Spawn{Role: "editor(plan-review)", Wave: [C1, C2]}`. `applySpawn` renders a correct brief (brief.go:120-128 reads `sp.Wave`) but calls `domain.Claim` with `PoolSnapshot: nil`, so `runs.pool_snapshot` is NULL.

PASS path: the Editor judges the wave sound and runs `mc editor plan-review --run <r> --initiative I --verdict pass`. `runPoolSnapshot` returns nil; `PassWaveReview` computes `open = [C1, C2]` from `openChildIDs`; `sameInt64Set([C1,C2], nil)` returns false at the `len(a) != len(b)` guard ⇒ `CodePoolMismatch`. The transaction rolls back — no `plan_reviewed = 1`, no `endRun`, no `releaseLease`. The run exits without an accepted terminal, so per §10 the reaper recovers it and charges `dispatch_retries` on I. This repeats every tick until `dispatch_retries` hits 0 and I is blocked. **No wave can ever be passed**, so `mc strategist wave` — the exact thing this ADR exists to unblock — still dead-ends, and Inv. 12's plan review can never actually admit a child to work.

SEND-BACK path (worse, because it is accepted): the Editor runs `--verdict send-back --reason "..."`. `SendBackWave` loops `for _, id := range snapshot` over nil — zero iterations — so C1 and C2 are never cancelled. It then writes the `wave.sent_back` activity row (initiative.go:158) and returns nil, so `endRun` + `releaseLease` succeed and the terminal is accepted. But the initiative is *not* drained: C1, C2 are still `archived = 0, plan_reviewed = 0`, so `planReviewPending(rec, I)` is still true on the next tick and `spawnFor` dispatches `editor(plan-review)` again over the same wave. Strategist(initiative) is never dispatched (the initiative never drains), so the objection is never answered. Because D2(d) puts the `seeded` initiative at stage_rank 2, above the proposed pool at rank 1, this livelock outranks and starves the ordinary contrastive pool pass indefinitely — a spin with no reaper backstop, since each run terminates cleanly.

raised.title: applySpawn never persists Spawn.Wave, so runs.pool_snapshot is NULL on every plan-review run — the pass arm is unreachable and the send-back arm is a no-op
raised.claim: ADR-020 D4 requires applySpawn to pass "whichever of the two the Editor mode populated into ClaimArgs.PoolSnapshot". The implementation gates the snapshot write on RoleEditor only, so a RoleEditorPlanReview spawn claims with PoolSnapshot=nil and runs.pool_snapshot stays NULL. Every plan-review terminal then reads back an empty snapshot: `pass` always fails pool-mismatch (the wave can never be passed, so no wave child ever dispatches), and `send-back` reports success while cancelling nothing.
raised.failure_scenario:
Verified by driving the real production path (verbs.Dispatch -> verbs.EditorPlanReview) against a real spine. Seed initiative id=1 status='seeded' with two born-unreviewed children id=2,3.

Tick 1 — `Dispatch(db)` emits `role=editor(plan-review)`, and the pure layer decided `Spawn.Wave = [2 3]`, but the spawn effect reports `pool_ids=[]` and `SELECT pool_snapshot FROM runs WHERE id=<run>` returns NULL (valid=false).

pass arm: `EditorPlanReview(..., Verdict:"pass")` -> `the wave changed under review: snapshot [], live open children [2 3] (ADR-020 D5)`. The pass is unreachable for ANY wave, so plan_reviewed never reaches 1, childGate never opens, and no wave child is ever dispatched. The run exits without an accepted terminal, so per §10 the reaper charges dispatch_retries on the initiative each round and blocks it at 0. `mc strategist wave` — the whole lane this ADR exists to unblock — dead-ends exactly as before.

send-back arm (worse, silent): `EditorPlanReview(..., Verdict:"send-back", Reason:"...")` -> err=nil, lease released, `wave.sent_back` written — but open children = 2, not 0. D5's postcondition ("The initiative now has zero open children => drained") fails, so planReviewPending stays true and the next tick dispatches another editor(plan-review). Three consecutive ticks each dispatched a fresh plan review, each send-back returned success, the wave stayed intact, and wave.sent_back rows accumulated 1 -> 2 -> 3: an unbounded live-lock burning Editor runs, distinct from and far tighter than sharp edge 2's acknowledged replan loop (which at least destroys and rebirths a wave).

raised.title: SendBackWave asserts no postcondition, converting a degenerate snapshot into a silent success instead of failing closed
raised.claim: SendBackWave derives everything it destroys from `snapshot` and never checks that the initiative actually drained. An empty or degenerate snapshot therefore cancels nothing, writes the wave.sent_back activity row anyway, and returns nil — releasing the lease on a wave that is still open and unreviewed. This is fail-open, contrary to the ADR's stated posture, and is what turns the finding above from a loud failure into a silent live-lock. It needs its own fix: repairing applySpawn removes today's trigger but leaves the fail-open latent for any future path that yields a NULL/partial snapshot (e.g. a run row written outside applySpawn, or a future D7 re-snapshot regression).
raised.failure_scenario: Same repro as above, send_back subtree: `EditorPlanReview(db, id, PlanReviewArgs{Run: runID, Initiative: 1, Verdict: "send-back", Reason: "wave is incoherent"})` returns err=nil and releases the lease, while `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tasks WHERE initiative_id=1 AND archived=0` = 2 and `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM activity WHERE kind='wave.sent_back'` = 1. The Editor is told its send-back landed; the spine still holds the wave it just refused. Because the arm asserts nothing, no error surfaces at any layer — the substrate is not involved (no illegal state is attempted, so no trigger can catch it), which is precisely why the storage fence cannot backstop this one. A cheap fix that fails closed for the right reason: after the loop, re-read openChildIDs and refuse (never partially apply) unless it is empty — i.e. assert D5's own postcondition rather than trusting the snapshot.

verdicts.reasoning:
CONFIRMED by direct reproduction, not inspection alone.

1. Signature change is real. `mc/domain/task.go:426`: `func Cancel(ctx context.Context, q Q, taskID int64, reason string, actor string) error`. At the range base, `git show 4618a23:mc/domain/task.go` line 421 reads `func Cancel(ctx context.Context, q Q, taskID int64, reason string) error` — 4 params. So the range widened it.

2. Exactly one 4-arg survivor repo-wide. `grep -rn 'Cancel(ctx' --include='*.go'` returns 14 call sites; 13 pass an actor (verbs/task.go:146, domain/task.go:456, domain/initiative.go:155, plus the 10 test sites the raiser listed). The sole holdout is `mc/property/lifecycle_nightly_test.go:1159`: `err = domain.Cancel(ctx, q, id, reason)`.

3. The range never touched the file. `git diff --stat 4618a23..2e289e5 -- mc/property/` is EMPTY, while `git show 4618a23:mc/property/lifecycle_nightly_test.go` already had the 4-arg call. It compiled before and does not now — this range introduced the break.

4. I compiled it. Go is not on PATH (mise-managed at ~/.local/share/mise/installs/go/1.24.5); module root is /mc, not repo root. From mc/: `go vet -tags nightly ./property/` →
   vet: property/lifecycle_nightly_test.go:1159:43: not enough arguments in call to domain.Cancel
     have (context.Context, domain.Q, int64, string)
     want (context.Context, domain.Q, int64, string, string)
   `go test -tags nightly -run XXXNONE ./property/` → "FAIL mc/property [build failed]". Failure scenario reproduced verbatim.

5. The fast-lane blind spot is real. `go vet ./...` (no tag) exits 0 — green. The gate is `mc/property/lifecycle_nightly_test.go:1` `//go:build nightly`, so the AGENTS.md §3 every-commit fast lane cannot see it.

6. Blast radius is wider than claimed: `dispatch_nightly_test.go` is also `package property` under the same tag, so BOTH nightly differentials — not just lifecycle — fail to build. ADR-020 D5:554-555 explicitly named `lifecycle_nightly_test.go` among the owed sites, so this is an unmet obligation of the ADR being implemented.

7. Fix verified, not assumed: patching 1159 to pass "operator" makes `go vet -tags nightly ./property/` exit 0. Tree restored clean afterward.

DEFLATION I owe the record: the raiser's claim that the dead arm "would otherwise have checked the activity row's actor, precisely the Inv. 7 property this ADR widened" is overstated. The model arm at 1170 hard-codes `INSERT INTO activity (actor, ...) VALUES ('operator', ...)`, and the fix passes "operator" on the impl side — both sides agree on operator, so this arm never exercises the new "editor" actor (domain/initiative.go:155) regardless. The build break is genuine; that specific coverage rationale is not.

Severity major, on my own read rather than the raiser's: no invariant is violated and no runtime/product code is wrong (blast radius is test-only, one-token fix, which argues minor). What carries it over the line is that a committed build break silently disables an entire verification package — the nightly differential lattice that AGENTS.md §3 says runs at phase completion. It falsifies the load-bearing claim that the lattice is green over Cancel, and it is latent: invisible until the next nightly invocation, when it will be misattributed to whatever change is in flight rather than to 2e289e5.
verdicts.recommended_action:
Two things, one tactical and one systemic.

Tactical (one line, verified to compile): at mc/property/lifecycle_nightly_test.go:1159 change `domain.Cancel(ctx, q, id, reason)` to `domain.Cancel(ctx, q, id, reason, "operator")` — matching the model arm's hard-coded `VALUES ('operator', ...)` at line 1170. Then run, from the mc/ module root, `go test -tags nightly ./property/` to confirm the lattice actually passes and not merely compiles. Note for whoever picks this up: `go` is not on the default PATH; use `mise exec -- go`, and the module root is mc/, not the repo root.

Systemic (the reason this got through): the fast lane vets only the default tag set, so tag-gated packages rot invisibly between phase completions. Add a compile-only guard to the every-commit fast lane — `go vet -tags nightly ./...` — which is Docker-free and fast, so it does not violate the AGENTS.md §3 rule that keeps the Docker-dependent suites out of inner loops. That catches build breaks in nightly code on the commit that causes them, while still deferring the expensive nightly *run* to phase completion.

Separately, do not credit this arm with Inv. 7 actor coverage. Once compiling, it exercises only the "operator" actor. If the ADR-020 "editor" send-back path (mc/domain/initiative.go:155) is meant to be covered by the differential, that needs a new arm whose model side expects `actor = 'editor'` — worth logging as its own item rather than assuming the fix restores it.

verdicts.reasoning:
Verified every link against primary sources; the finding holds exactly as raised.

1. ADR instruction exists and is unimplemented. docs/adr/020-initiative-wave-plan-review.md:376-379 verbatim: "`dispatch.Spawn` gains a distinct `Wave []int64` field ... and `applySpawn` (dispatchverb.go:359) passes whichever of the two the Editor mode populated into `ClaimArgs.PoolSnapshot`." I read the full `git diff 4618a23..2e289e5 -- mc/verbs/dispatchverb.go`: it touches ONLY `loadRecords` (adding the `plan_reviewed` projection). applySpawn is untouched.

2. The gate is a plain string compare against a distinct constant. mc/verbs/dispatchverb.go:359-367 reads verbatim as quoted: `var pool []int64` / `if sp.Role == dispatch.RoleEditor {` / `pool = sp.ProposedPool`. mc/dispatch/dispatch.go:231 `RoleEditor Role = "editor"` vs dispatch.go:239 `RoleEditorPlanReview Role = "editor(plan-review)"`. Distinct values, so `pool` stays nil for a plan-review spawn, and dispatchverb.go:375 passes `PoolSnapshot: pool` (nil).

3. Nothing carries Wave to the claim. My own grep for `Wave` over non-test .go files: sole writer mc/dispatch/dispatch.go:690 `sp.Wave = wave(rec, t.ID)`; sole reader mc/verbs/brief.go:121. No third reference.

4. applySpawn is the ONLY claim path. mc/verbs/dispatchverb.go:299-304: `case dispatch.KindSpawn:` -> `return applySpawn(ctx, q, now, a.Spawn, tun, route)`. No alternate branch.

5. nil => NULL. mc/domain/lease.go:81-93: `var pool any` / `if a.PoolSnapshot != nil { ... pool = string(b) }` then INSERT with `pool` still nil => pool_snapshot NULL. mc/verbs/roles.go:459-473 `runPoolSnapshot` scans into `sql.NullString`; invalid => returns nil slice.

6. REACHABILITY PROVEN BY THE REPO'S OWN TEST — this is where most findings die, and this one does not. mc/verbs/dispatchverb_test.go:538-544 drives `dispatch.Decide` on REAL spine rows (loadRecords over a real DB seeded by dvUnreviewedWave) and asserts `a.Spawn.Role == RoleEditorPlanReview` with `a.Spawn.Wave == []int64{2, 3}`. No CHECK/trigger/fence blocks the state. baseRole() (mc/verbs/verbs.go:113-118) strips the mode to "editor", so the runs.role CHECK admits the row — the claim succeeds and silently writes NULL.

7. PASS arm can NEVER succeed (not an edge case). planReviewPending (mc/dispatch/dispatch.go:742-752) returns true only if some child has `!c.Archived && !c.PlanReviewed`, so openChildIDs (mc/domain/initiative.go:175-191, `archived = 0`) is always non-empty when a plan review dispatches. snapshot is nil (len 0) => sameInt64Set (initiative.go:200-202) fails at `if len(a) != len(b) { return false }` => CodePoolMismatch (initiative.go:111-114). inTx rolls back: no plan_reviewed=1, no endRun, no releaseLease.

8. SEND-BACK arm is an accepted no-op that livelocks. initiative.go:149-152 `for _, id := range snapshot` over nil = zero iterations, no Cancel; then recordWaveEvent("wave.sent_back") and `return nil`, so endRun + releaseLease succeed (roles.go:446-450). Children remain archived=0, plan_reviewed=0 => planReviewPending still true => re-dispatch next tick, with NO reaper backstop since each run terminates cleanly.

9. Tests are blind, as claimed. dvAssertEffect's KindSpawn arm (dispatchverb_test.go:152-169) asserts role, subject_id, and `got["pool_ids"]` against `want.Spawn.ProposedPool` — never Wave, never pool_snapshot. claimPlanReview (mc/cmd/mc/cli_test.go:4912-4919) hand-inserts `INSERT INTO runs (... role, ... pool_snapshot) VALUES (?, 'pipeline', 'editor', ...)` with a populated snapshot, bypassing applySpawn entirely.

SEVERITY (judged independently, and the raiser if anything understated it): major. childGate (dispatch.go:756-758) `return t.InitiativeID == nil || t.PlanReviewed` makes EVERY initiative child undispatchable until plan_reviewed=1, and plan_reviewed is only ever written by PassWaveReview's loop — the arm that can never fire. The whole initiative pipeline is wedged and Inv. 12's plan review can never admit a child to work.

LIMITATION (stated honestly, does not soften the verdict): no Go toolchain exists in this sandbox (`go` not found; searched /usr/local/go, /opt/homebrew, ~/go/bin, mdfind), so I authored a reproduction test driving the real Dispatch(db) and asserting runs.pool_snapshot, but could not execute it, and removed the file. The chain is a sequence of unconditional, statically determined steps (a constant string comparison, a nil check, a length check) with no runtime variance, so static verification is conclusive here.

CONFIRMED — I reproduced both arms myself by driving the real production path, not by trusting the raiser's evidence.

**The code, re-read directly.** `git diff 4618a23..2e289e5 -- mc/verbs/dispatchverb.go` touches ONLY `loadRecords` (adding `plan_reviewed`). `applySpawn` was never modified. mc/verbs/dispatchverb.go:361-367 verbatim:
```go
	var pool []int64
	if sp.Role == dispatch.RoleEditor {
		pool = sp.ProposedPool
		if pool == nil {
			pool = []int64{}
		}
	}
```
`grep -rn "PoolSnapshot" mc --include="*.go" | grep -v _test.go` proves dispatchverb.go:375 (`PoolSnapshot: pool`) is the SOLE production writer — there is no other path. `RoleEditorPlanReview` appears in brief.go and directives.go but nowhere in dispatchverb.go. mc/domain/lease.go:82 `if a.PoolSnapshot != nil {` then simply never writes the column. The pure layer does populate it (dispatch.go:690 `sp.Wave = wave(rec, t.ID)`), so the value is computed and discarded at the seam.

**Reachability: fully reachable — I drove it, not hypothesized it.** I wrote a test calling the real entrypoints `verbs.Dispatch` (which calls `applySpawn`) then `verbs.EditorPlanReview` against a real spine, seeding initiative id=1 'seeded' with two born-unreviewed children 2,3. Output:
- Tick 1: `action=spawn role=editor(plan-review) pool_ids=[]`; `SELECT pool_snapshot FROM runs` → `valid=false` (NULL).
- pass arm: `err = the wave changed under review: snapshot [], live open children [2 3] (ADR-020 D5)`; children marked plan_reviewed=1: **0**.
- send-back arm, 3 consecutive ticks: each `err=<nil>` (reports success), `open_children=2` every round (want 0), `wave.sent_back` rows accumulate 1→2→3. Each tick re-dispatches a fresh `editor(plan-review)`. Silent unbounded live-lock.

**Root cause proven by fix.** Adding the `RoleEditorPlanReview → pool = sp.Wave` arm flips both: `pool_snapshot valid=true value="[2,3]"`, pass `err=<nil>`, 2 children marked; send-back drains so round 2 dispatches `strategist(initiative)` — exactly D5's postcondition. No other change needed.

**Why tests miss it (masking confirmed).** mc/cmd/mc/cli_test.go:4914 — the helper's own comment: "claimPlanReview **fakes** the claim transaction", hand-writing `INSERT INTO runs (... pool_snapshot) VALUES (...)`, bypassing applySpawn. The dispatch-side test (dispatchverb_test.go:538-544) stops at `Decide`, asserting `a.Spawn.Wave` from the pure layer. Both sides tested; the seam is not. I ran the full fast suite with the bug live: **all packages green**. (Note: two foreign scratch files from concurrent reviewers, zz_skeptic2/zz_judge3_test.go, broke the build; I moved them aside and restored them. Working tree left as found.)

**Against the ADR.** D4 (line 379-381) requires: "`applySpawn` (dispatchverb.go:359) passes whichever of the two the Editor mode populated into `ClaimArgs.PoolSnapshot`." The code honors this for one of the two modes only. This is a code-vs-ADR deviation, not a re-litigation of D4.

**Severity: major**, independent of the raiser's claim. (1) It falsifies a load-bearing claim in the ADR's own Status section — "D1–D5 are green on the fast lane and `mc strategist wave` is CLI-wired — the Parked entry this ADR exists to close is retired." The pass arm is unreachable for ANY wave, so `plan_reviewed` never reaches 1, `childGate` never opens, and no wave child ever dispatches: the lane this ADR exists to unblock still dead-ends. Inv. 12 is not delivered for waves. (2) Per §10, the pass run exits without an accepted terminal, so the reaper charges `dispatch_retries` on the initiative each round and blocks it at 0 — a wedge. (3) The send-back live-lock is distinct from and far tighter than acknowledged sharp edge 2 (which at least destroys and rebirths a wave); here nothing changes state, so it burns Editor runs unboundedly while reporting success. Ironically this is the same class of seam bug the ADR's Status credits a differential with catching on the read side (`loadRecords` never read `plan_reviewed`).

Verified every link myself against the committed tree at 2e289e5; the finding is accurate in every particular and understates the send-back damage.

THE DEFECT. mc/verbs/dispatchverb.go:359-367 is the sole writer of the snapshot, and it reads verbatim as quoted: `var pool []int64` / `if sp.Role == dispatch.RoleEditor { pool = sp.ProposedPool ... }`. mc/dispatch/dispatch.go:231 (`RoleEditor Role = "editor"`) and :239 (`RoleEditorPlanReview Role = "editor(plan-review)"`) are distinct constants, so the guard is false for a plan review and `pool` stays nil. `git grep -n "PoolSnapshot:" 2e289e5 -- "mc/*.go" | grep -v _test` returns exactly one writer: dispatchverb.go:375. mc/domain/lease.go:82 `if a.PoolSnapshot != nil` then drops it, so the INSERT binds NULL.

I CLOSED AN ESCAPE HATCH THE RAISER DID NOT CHECK. If the schema defaulted or backfilled the column the finding would die. It does not: schema.sql:690 is bare `pool_snapshot TEXT,` with no DEFAULT, and the only triggers on `runs` are `runs_no_delete` (:708) and `runs_session_locators_immutable` (:714) — neither touches it. The NULL survives to storage.

REACHABILITY — I tried to kill this and could not. The arm genuinely fires: loadRecords DOES project the flag (dispatchverb.go:195 selects `plan_reviewed`, :222 `t.PlanReviewed = planReviewed == 1`), and the project's own test at dispatchverb_test.go:539-544 proves `Decide` returns RoleEditorPlanReview with Wave [2 3] from real spine rows. applyAction routes KindSpawn -> applySpawn unconditionally; it is the sole claim path. planReviewPending (dispatch.go) REQUIRES an open child with `!c.PlanReviewed`, so `open` is guaranteed non-empty — which guarantees the length mismatch rather than leaving it to chance. No trigger, CHECK, or fence upstream makes the state unconstructible.

EVIDENCE THE RAISER MISSED, AND IT IS THE SHARPEST. `sp.Wave` is consumed at exactly ONE non-test site: brief.go:121 `for _, id := range sp.Wave`, which renders it into the brief. So the Editor really is shown wave [7,8] and judges it, while the spine records NULL. The value exists in the brief and nowhere in storage — the coverage check compares [] against [7 8].

BOTH ARMS, TRACED. pass: initiative.go:111 `!sameInt64Set(open, snapshot)` -> sameInt64Set(:200-202) returns false on `len(a) != len(b)` (2 vs 0) -> CodePoolMismatch. Run exits with no accepted terminal, lease rules charge dispatch_retries on the initiative, it eventually blocks out. Unreachable in production. send-back: initiative.go:149 `for _, id := range snapshot` over nil = zero iterations -> cancels nothing -> writes wave.sent_back -> returns nil.

I CORRECT THE RAISER IN THE FINDING'S FAVOR: the send-back loop is worse than described. roles.go:447 calls `endRun(ctx, q, a.Run, "completed")`, so the run SUCCEEDS and no dispatch_retries is charged — there is no budget that ever blocks it out. planReviewPending stays true forever, so spawnFor always picks RoleEditorPlanReview over RoleStrategistInitiative and the lane spins truly unbounded. ADR-020 sharp edge 2 accepted an unbounded send-back/replan loop as pathological-AGENT behavior; that acceptance does not cover this, because this loop is deterministic, fires on every send-back regardless of agent quality, and no replan ever occurs.

TEST BLINDNESS AND DEVIATION LOG, both verified. `git grep -n "applySpawn" 2e289e5 -- "*_test.go"` returns NOTHING — applySpawn has zero test coverage. cli_test.go:4913-4920 `claimPlanReview` states in its own comment that it "fakes the claim transaction" and hand-inserts the snapshot; dispatchverb_test.go:542 asserts only the pure `a.Spawn.Wave`. `git diff 4618a23..2e289e5 -- IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES.md` logs three unrelated items (the §4-class rule, the send-back cancellation widening, the scope matrix) and nothing about the snapshot write gap. Not logged.

LIMITATION, stated honestly: no Go toolchain exists on this machine (`go` absent from /usr/local/go, /opt/homebrew, ~/sdk and PATH), so I could not execute a demonstration test — I wrote one and removed it. This does not soften the verdict: the path is straight-line with a constant comparison, no interfaces, dynamic dispatch, or reflection, and each link is verified verbatim.

SEVERITY. Major: both arms of the terminal ADR-020 exists to build are non-functional, the wave lane wedges in exactly the way the ADR set out to unwedge, Inv. 12 for waves is unrealized in production, and the ADR's Status block claim that "D1-D5 are green" is falsified. The fast suite stays green throughout — this is the same write-side recurrence of the projection failure class the ADR's own Status celebrates catching on the read side.

CONFIRMED — I reproduced this end-to-end rather than reasoning about it, and every quoted snippet is verbatim-accurate.

**The seam is real.** `mc/verbs/dispatchverb.go:361-367` is verbatim as quoted, and `grep -rn "RoleEditor" --include="*.go" | grep -v _test.go` shows it is the ONLY non-test site mentioning `RoleEditor` without a companion `RoleEditorPlanReview` branch — `brief.go:111`/`brief.go:120` and `directives.go:45-46` both handle the pair. `grep -rn "pool_snapshot"` confirms `mc/domain/lease.go:81-93` is the sole writer of the column, and it turns a nil `PoolSnapshot` into a NULL (`var pool any; if a.PoolSnapshot != nil {...}`).

**The ADR is explicitly violated** (not my inference — D4's own text): "`dispatch.Spawn` gains a distinct `Wave []int64` field ... and `applySpawn` (dispatchverb.go:359) **passes whichever of the two the Editor mode populated** into `ClaimArgs.PoolSnapshot`." Only one of the two is passed.

**Reachability — proven, not argued.** Go isn't on PATH (it's under mise at `~/.local/share/mise/installs/go/1.24.5/bin`), and concurrent reviewer agents had left a broken test file in `mc/verbs/`, so I ran in a clean detached worktree at 2e289e5 to avoid touching their uncommitted work. Driving the real `dvDispatch`→`applySpawn` path over `dvUnreviewedWave`:
- `role=editor(plan-review) spawn.Wave=[2 3]` but `runs.pool_snapshot valid=false` — NULL, exactly as claimed. `pool_ids=[]` on the effect too.

Then driving the real `EditorPlanReview` terminal from that real run (not the hand-inserted row):
- (a) pass → `the wave changed under review: snapshot [], live open children [2 3] (ADR-020 D5)`; `plan_reviewed=1` count: **0**. Pass is unreachable for every wave.
- (b) send-back → **accepted** (`map[initiative:1 verdict:send-back]`, err=nil), children still open: **2**, `wave.sent_back` rows: 1. Next tick re-selects `role=editor(plan-review) wave=[2 3]` — the livelock, confirmed directly.

**Diagnosis confirmed by the fix.** Applying the proposed branch flips all three: `pool_snapshot="[2,3]"`; pass → `verdict:pass` with 2 children marked; send-back → 0 children open and next tick advances to `strategist(initiative)`. Full suite `go test ./...` is green BOTH with and without the fix — proving the seam is genuinely untested (as claimed: `cli_test.go:4913` `claimPlanReview` "fakes the claim transaction" with a hand-inserted `pool_snapshot`; `TestPlanReviewBrief` drives the real path but asserts only `got["brief"]`) and that no test encodes the buggy behavior.

**Consequence.** The child gate is real (`dispatch.go:550,644` skip unreviewed wave children), so with `plan_reviewed` permanently 0 no wave child is ever dispatchable, while `planReviewPending` stays true forever and re-spawns the review each tick. Since the lease is a single lock row (Inv. 4), the starvation plausibly extends past the wave lane to the whole pipeline. This is precisely the both-invisible-and-owed wedge ADR-020 exists to close, reintroduced one layer below the pure dispatch logic — and it is not sharp edge 2's bounded ping-pong, since no replan intervenes.

**One caveat, stated for honesty:** I did not independently verify the raiser's sub-claim in arm (a) that the reaper charges `dispatch_retries` and blocks I after three ticks. That detail is downstream color; it does not affect the verdict — the pool-mismatch refusal and the zero marking are directly confirmed.

Severity major on my own judgment: the ADR-020 mechanism is non-functional on every real dispatched plan review (100% of the product path), state advances impossibly, and the system livelocks — it falsifies the load-bearing claim that the wave is held to Inv. 12's bar.
verdicts.recommended_action:
Apply the proposed fix at mc/verbs/dispatchverb.go:361 — populate `pool` from `sp.Wave` for `dispatch.RoleEditorPlanReview` alongside the existing `RoleEditor` branch (nil-coalescing to `[]int64{}` so the column is `[]`, never NULL). Verified: this alone flips both arms green and leaves the full suite passing. Consider the `if/else if` or a switch on `sp.Role` so the two Editor modes are visibly exhaustive and a third mode cannot silently fall through to nil again.

Close the test gap, which is the more durable defect: the D5 terminal tests hand-insert `runs.pool_snapshot` (cli_test.go:4913 `claimPlanReview`, "fakes the claim transaction") while the D4 brief test (dispatchverb_test.go:582) drives the real `applySpawn` but asserts only on the brief — so nothing joins the two halves. Add a test that (1) asserts `runs.pool_snapshot` after a real `dvDispatch` plan-review spawn, and (2) drives `EditorPlanReview` from that real run rather than a hand-inserted row. Both arms should be covered: pass marks the wave, send-back archives it and lets the next tick advance to `strategist(initiative)`.

Also worth a look while in here: `poolIDs := sp.ProposedPool` at dispatchverb.go:382 emits `pool_ids: []` on the plan-review spawn effect. Not implicated in this wedge (the terminal reads the column, not the effect), so treat it as a separate judgment call about what the effect should advertise for the wave — but it has the same root cause and should not be fixed by reflex without deciding what run.json's `pool_ids` means for an `editor(plan-review)` run.

verdicts.reasoning:
CONFIRMED — I reproduced the exact scenario end-to-end, and every element of the claim holds.

**The quoted code is accurate.** mc/domain/initiative.go:149-159 is verbatim as cited: the loop is snapshot-driven, `continue`s on any non-live member, and falls through to `return recordWaveEvent(ctx, q, "wave.sent_back", initiativeID, reason)`. With `snapshot == nil` the body never executes. (One cite drift, immaterial: the contrasting `sameInt64Set` guard is at initiative.go:111-115, not 118-121.)

**The trigger is real and present today, not hypothetical.** I traced the snapshot's provenance myself. `dispatch.spawnFor` (dispatch.go:686-690) does populate the wave: `sp.Role = RoleEditorPlanReview; sp.Wave = wave(rec, t.ID)`. But `applySpawn` (mc/verbs/dispatchverb.go:361-367) gates the snapshot on the pool role alone:
    var pool []int64
    if sp.Role == dispatch.RoleEditor {
        pool = sp.ProposedPool
        ...
    }
`sp.Wave` is never read here — `grep -rn "\.Wave" mc/verbs/` shows its only consumer is brief.go:121-126 (rendering). So ClaimArgs.PoolSnapshot is nil, domain.Claim's `if a.PoolSnapshot != nil` (lease.go:82) is false, and runs.pool_snapshot is written NULL. runPoolSnapshot (roles.go:461-472) returns nil,nil on a NULL column — no error. ADR-020 D4 specifies the opposite ("applySpawn passes whichever of the two the Editor mode populated into ClaimArgs.PoolSnapshot"), so this is an unimplemented line, not a design choice.

**Repro** (Go test against a real spine via dvSpine/dvUnreviewedWave, dispatching for real then calling the terminal):
    dispatched role=editor(plan-review) run=140aedf094dab4c0 spawn.Wave=[2 3]
    runs.pool_snapshot: valid=false value=""
    EditorPlanReview(send-back) -> out=map[initiative:1 verdict:send-back] err=<nil>
    run outcome="completed"; lease still held=false
    POSTCONDITIONS: open unreviewed children=2, wave.sent_back rows=1, held leases=0
    NEXT TICK: role=editor(plan-review) subject=1
The wave [2 3] is computed by the pure layer then discarded at the seam. The send-back returns nil, stamps the run completed, releases the lease, and writes the wave.sent_back row — while both children remain open and plan_reviewed = 0. Nothing is cancelled. The next tick re-dispatches editor(plan-review) on the same initiative: a live-lock that charges no dispatch_retries (the terminal was *accepted*), so it never self-blocks and burns a fresh Editor run every tick.

**The asymmetry the raiser describes is exactly right.** My control run confirms the sibling arm fails closed on identical input:
    EditorPlanReview(pass) -> err=the wave changed under review: snapshot [], live open children [2 3] (ADR-020 D5)
Same NULL snapshot; `pass` refuses loudly via sameInt64Set, `send-back` reports success. No substrate backstop can catch this — no illegal state is attempted, so no trigger, CHECK, or fence fires. The storage fence genuinely cannot reach it.

**Where I depart from the raiser: on independent severity.** The root cause is the applySpawn seam (their sibling finding). I checked whether a degenerate snapshot is reachable *with applySpawn repaired*, and it is not: planReviewPending guarantees >=1 open unreviewed child, so wave() is non-empty; and ADR-020 D6 holds by construction — BirthWave refuses to insert while any child is open (initiative.go:56-66), so no child can appear mid-review and snapshot is always a superset of the live open set. The loop then cancels every live member and the postcondition holds. The genuinely degenerate case the ADR *does* license — an operator cancelling all children mid-review — is handled correctly: live set empty, loop skips both, and "zero open children => drained" is already true.

So today's wedge belongs to applySpawn; attributing it to this finding double-counts. What survives independently is true and worth fixing on its own: the arm asserts none of D5's own stated postcondition ("The initiative now has zero open children => drained"), so any future path yielding a partial snapshot degrades silently rather than loudly. That is a real defect with bounded blast radius once the seam is fixed — minor, not major, and a fail-closed hardening rather than the primary repair.
